answer:
**Iqbal's "Khitāb ba Jawānān-e-Islām" (Bang-E-Dara Part 3, ch. 13)** is a complete poetic address in the second person ("you," "your"), opening with a rhetorical question to provoke self-examination and implicitly demanding revival through action.[^1] The speaker contrasts the addressees' glorious ancestors—world-conquering Arabs—with their current inertia ("you are all talk, they were all action; you are static, they were ever-moving"), laments lost heritage (e.g., ancestral books now in Europe), and urges recovery beyond mere political rule.[^2]

**Cynewulf's epilogues**, embedded as signed runic closings in *Juliana* (ch. 1), *Elene* (ch. 1), and *Andreas* (ch. 1), shift from narrative to first-person pleas directed at "every person of humankind" who recites the poem.[^3][^4][^5] He demands intercessory prayer ("pray to the Molder that the Helm of the heavens grant me help") and ties it to eschatological judgment, warning of fire-trials where deeds (including remembrance) determine fate.[^6]

Both authors rely on second-person address for immediacy and imperative force but hedge extensively with explanation: Iqbal via historical exempla and diagnostics of decline; Cynewulf via doctrinal exposition of divine judgment and mercy. Neither trusts bare imperatives alone, embedding demands in vivid, justificatory rhetoric to motivate.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 13 "Khitab ba Jawanan-e-Islam" [p-0291d8](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/#p-0291d8).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-365462](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/13-khitab-ba-jawanan-e-islam/translation/#p-365462).

[^3]: Cynewulf, *Juliana*, ch. 1, closing [p-1f8ce8](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-1f8ce8).

[^4]: Cynewulf, *Elene*, ch. 1, closing [p-dcbbfe](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-dcbbfe).

[^5]: Cynewulf, *Andreas*, ch. 1, [p-be1571](/works/cynewulf-andreas-07b573/01/translation/#p-be1571).

[^6]: *Juliana*, ch. 1 [p-1f8ce8](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-1f8ce8); *Elene*, ch. 1 [p-dcbbfe](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-dcbbfe).
